persona.name: Roberto Fuentes

occupation.title: Community Garden Coordinator (retired)
occupation.organization: Marana Parks & Recreation (part-time, volunteer-equivalent); retired from USPS after 32-year career as a mail carrier
occupation.description: Spent 32 years walking 8–12 miles per day as a mail carrier in Tucson's south side. Now retired and coordinates a community garden in Marana three mornings a week — watering schedules, soil amendments, neighbor volunteer coordination. Jogs partly because the absence of his walking routes left a hole in his daily structure.

persona.education: High school diploma, Sunnyside High School, Tucson; several community college courses in business and Spanish literature
long_term_goals: Jog consistently three mornings a week without significant joint pain through his 60s
Lose 20 pounds and keep his type 2 diabetes in the controlled range without medication increase
Teach his granddaughter Elena to run a mile
Expand the community garden to include a youth plot for neighborhood children
persona.style: Reserved and careful in new situations; opens up gradually over time and with people who've earned his trust. Speaks slowly and deliberately, choosing each word with intention. Prefers to explain a point through personal experience rather than abstract argument. Has a quiet dignity that coexists with genuine warmth when he's comfortable. Does not perform enthusiasm he doesn't feel.

traits: Cautious and deliberate — will not be rushed into a decision
Loyal to what has worked; change requires a compelling real-world reason
Physically capable and quietly proud of his fitness history
Family-anchored — nearly every preference or habit connects back to family context
Skeptical of new or trendy products; trusts the familiar and tested

interests: Morning jogs as retirement health routine
Community gardening (vegetables, native plants, some fruit trees)
Watching Liga MX and the Mexican national team
Spending time with his granddaughter Elena
likes: Shoes that 'just work' without needing to think about them
Returning to a brand that did not disappoint him previously
Early morning walks or jogs before Tucson heat arrives
Straightforward, honest product communication — no buzzwords
New Balance or Saucony for reliable, unfussy wear
dislikes: Shoes with thick cushioning that disconnects him from the ground — after 32 years of mail-carrier walking, he likes to feel the pavement
Complicated lacing systems — he wants to put them on and go
Being pushed toward trendy shoes by salespeople half his age
Spending over $100 on shoes unless he has a clear reason
Any product that hurt his feet and was not returnable

beliefs: You trust what you've tested yourself, not what an advertisement tells you
Family and land are what matter — everything else is peripheral
Simple and honest beats clever every time, in products and in people
Consistency in small habits beats dramatic interventions
skills: 30+ years of daily walking on varied terrain — intuitive footwear evaluator
Community garden coordination and volunteer management
Basic home electrical and plumbing repair

general: Jogs 3 mornings per week (Mon/Wed/Fri) at 6:00am before Tucson heat
Has bought the same brand and model twice in a row — will not change without a concrete problem
Was disappointed by one pair of 'premium' shoes that caused heel blisters; now deeply suspicious of any shoe he can't evaluate on a real walk first
Does not research shoes online; buys from a local shoe store in Tucson where a staff member he trusts works

morning: Coffee with Maria before going out
2.5–3 mile jog through the neighborhood before the heat builds
workday: Lunch at home, usually leftovers or a simple torta
Afternoon is unstructured — errands, home projects, television
evening: Dinner with Maria — traditional home-cooked meals
Watches fútbol or a Mexican telenovela
Elena visits 2–3 evenings per week when her parents work late
weekend: Elena runs around the block with him most Saturday mornings
Extended family gatherings at least once a month
Mass on Sundays, then slow morning at home

health.physical: Naturally fit baseline from 32 years of daily walking carrier routes. Managing mild arthritis in his left ankle and type 2 diabetes (controlled through diet and jogging). Carries some excess weight from retirement reduction in daily activity; working on it. Foot sensitivity is real — bad shoe choices cause disproportionate problems given his ankle condition.
health.mental: Jogging and the community garden together form his retirement structure. On days he skips both, he feels unmoored and slightly irritable. Maria has learned to flag this. Connection to his grandchildren and the garden community are significant emotional anchors.

relationships.name: Maria Fuentes
relationships.description: Married 30 years. Manages his diabetes diet with firm care and mild nagging. Goes for slow walks with him on Sunday mornings — their shared quiet time.

relationships.name: Tomás
relationships.description: Works in distribution logistics in Phoenix. Calls every Sunday. Bought Roberto his current running shoes last Father's Day — the good pair, the one that actually fit.

relationships.name: Elena
relationships.description: Eight years old. Runs around the block with Roberto on weekend mornings and asks approximately 40 questions per mile.
